dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Local control panel for child care booking automation.
dashboard.session_done_sub: Submit was successful. Start a new session!
dashboard.new_session_confirm: Clear current form & mapping and start a new session?
dashboard.card_form_desc: Parse a Google Form URL to detect fields, entry IDs, and date options.
dashboard.card_config_desc: Edit default values such as child name, parent, work unit, and more.
dashboard.card_status_desc: Check whether the scheduler is active, has submitted, or failed.
dashboard.card_logs_desc: Audit dry-runs, submits, errors, and cancellations.
dashboard.dev_desc: Runs the submit scheduler with attempts scheduled {delay} seconds after activation. Form and config are identical to a real submit. Use this to verify the submit flow without waiting until 13:00.
dashboard.dev_confirm: Run TEST submit scheduler? The form will be sent after the configured delay.

form.title: Form Parser
form.subtitle: Enter a Google Form URL to detect fields, entry IDs, and date options.

mapping.title: Review Field Mapping
mapping.subtitle: Review and confirm the mapping of Google Form fields to internal fields before dry-run.
mapping.match_hint: The Internal Field column can be changed if the automatic result is wrong. Fields with status unmapped must be selected manually before continuing.

config.title: Config Editor
config.subtitle: Edit default values used when filling the Google Form.
config.saved_incomplete: Incomplete fields: {fields} — dry-run cannot be run yet.
config.booking_hint: Select one or more dates from the options available in the Google Form.
config.attempt_hint: One time per line, format HH:MM:SS. At least 1 required.

dryrun.title: Dry Run
dryrun.subtitle: Simulate form fill without sending data to Google Form.
dryrun.ready_desc: The system will build the form payload from the confirmed config and mappings, then display a preview without sending anything to Google.
dryrun.running: Dry-run running — {run_id}. Terminal opened in background.

submit_prepare.title: Prepare Submit
submit_prepare.subtitle: Review the summary before running the real submit scheduler.
submit_prepare.dryrun_warning: After editing config, dry-run must be re-run before submit can proceed.
submit_prepare.confirm_desc: The PowerShell scheduler will run as a background process. The form will be sent to Google at {times}. Make sure the laptop does not sleep during that window.
submit_prepare.confirm_warning: This action cannot be undone after the first attempt is sent.

status.title: Scheduler Status
status.subtitle: Scheduler condition and latest run.
status.no_log: No logs yet. Run a dry-run to start recording.

logs.title: Logs & History
logs.subtitle: Audit all dry-runs, submits, errors, and cancellations — grouped by form session.
logs.empty: No runs yet. Run a dry-run to start recording history.

reset.title: Reset Data
reset.subtitle: Delete session data, mapping, run history, or scheduler lock. This action cannot be undone.
reset.form_confirm: Delete form profile and all mappings?
reset.runs_desc: There are run records and logs stored in data/runs/ and data/logs/.
reset.runs_confirm: Delete all run history and logs?
reset.lock_active: ⚠ Active lock. Delete only if the scheduler is truly no longer running.

help.title: User Guide
help.subtitle: Workflow and safety rules for the Tara Caraka Ceria control panel.
help.step1_desc: Enter the latest Google Form URL. The system will detect all fields, entry IDs, field types, and booking date options.
help.step2_desc: Review and confirm the mapping of form labels to internal fields. Fields with status unmapped must be selected manually.
help.step3_desc: Fill or update default values: child name, birth info, age, gender, religion, allergy, parent name, work unit, phone, address, and select booking dates.
help.step4_desc: Simulate form fill. The payload is built and displayed, but nothing is sent to Google. Do this every time config or form changes.
help.step5_desc: Review the final summary: endpoint, fields, values, attempt schedule. Make sure everything is correct before proceeding.
help.step6_desc: Click the red button to run the PowerShell scheduler as a background process. The form will be sent at 12:59:58, 12:59:59, and 13:00:00.
help.step7_desc: Track whether the scheduler is active, completed, or failed. The latest log is shown directly on the status page.
help.safety1: Submit always requires explicit confirmation — no auto-submit.
help.safety2: Use Dry Run every time config or form changes before a real submit.
help.safety3: Only one scheduler may be active at a time — the system will reject if a lock already exists.
help.safety4: Do not close the laptop or let it sleep during the submit window (around 12:55–13:01).
help.safety5: To cancel the scheduler, use the Cancel Scheduler button on the Status page — only safe before 12:59:58.
help.safety6: All actions (dry-run, submit, cancel) are recorded in Logs & History.
help.reparse1: Google Form is replaced with a new link or version.
help.reparse2: Field labels changed or a typo was fixed by the form admin.
help.reparse4: Entry IDs changed (usually when the form is recreated).
help.reparse_footer: After re-parsing, always do Review Mapping and Dry Run before submitting.
